clusters.root_cause_summary: The episode is natively solvable — candidate_0 succeeded via passive climb_stair during explore mode — but candidate_2's hysteresis patch converted the working passive climb path into a get_close_to_stair→look_for_downstair oscillation, tripling floor reinits (3→6) and exhausting the step budget. The toilet is in the same 2D-connected component as the agent start.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster; no cross-scene comparison possible. Unique in being solved by baseline candidate_0 and broken purely by a harness patch introduced to address a different failure class. Failure tag 'never_saw_target_traveled_stairs_feasible' confirms this is a regression, not a structural geometry failure.
clusters.transfer_notes: Demonstrates that any patch introducing explicit stair-seeking behavior risks disrupting passive climb_stair paths that already exist in baseline. All future candidates targeting the navmesh-disconnection clusters must use mL8ThkuaVTM as a regression canary: the mode sequence climb_stair(91-102) must remain reachable and floor reinits must stay ≤3.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: revert_hysteresis_patch_and_preserve_passive_climb_stair_detection_during_explore: remove or guard the hysteresis patch so passive climb_stair during explore mode is not intercepted; candidate_0 already proves this path succeeds with no other changes required, making this a near-zero-risk fix

clusters.root_cause_summary: Stair centroids in both scenes lie in navmesh-disconnected components (Reach_stair_centroid=False on every approach attempt); all nine prior interventions targeted look_for_downstair, which exits naturally in 2–12 steps, while the actual prolonged stall occurs in get_close_to_stair (75+ steps). Habitat's pathfinder.find_path() returns false-feasible via node snapping, masking the true connectivity failure.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical: stair centroid unreachable across all candidates, all look_for_downstair interventions are behavioral no-ops (mode exits naturally before any budget or counter fires), actual stall is in get_close_to_stair, identical fingerprints across ≥5 candidates each. Different: q3zU7Yy5E5s stall begins ~step 179 with 5 distinct centroids tested across a ~0.9m region; qyAac8rV8Zk stall is steps 164–239 (75 steps) with min_dis_to_downstair oscillating 159–175 without convergence; centroid coordinates differ but both are in sub-1m navmesh-disconnected stairwell regions.
clusters.transfer_notes: candidate_5 in q3zU7Yy5E5s extended look_for_downstair to steps 70–115 (vs 70–81 baseline), yielding explore(116–210) before re-entering get_close_to_stair — confirming that even an additional ~100 steps of floor exploration does not prevent cycling back to the stalled FSM mode. Both scenes confirm pathfinder.find_path() node-snapping is unreliable for disconnection detection; navmesh island_radius on the snapped point is the correct API. The gate in candidate_8 either fell through try/except or returned false-feasible via snapping in both scenes, making pathfinder.find_path() a ruled-out diagnostic primitive for this cluster.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: step_budget_or_reach_centroid_false_count_exit_on_get_close_to_stair combined with island_radius_connectivity_check: within get_close_to_stair, after N=10 consecutive Reach_stair_centroid=False (or T=25 steps), call pathfinder.island_radius(snap_point) on the snapped centroid — if the island radius is smaller than the agent's current island radius, declare the stair infeasible, permanently disable it, and trigger frontier regeneration with a wider search radius to recover remaining episode budget

clusters.root_cause_summary: Stairs are successfully traversed at step 198 (climb_stair success, floor_step resets to 0), but the goal room on floor 2 lies in a navmesh-disconnected component unreachable from the stair landing; only 2 near-landing frontiers exist on floor 2, exhausted in ~16 floor_steps, after which T4_NOQUIT rescues both find an empty frontier pool.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single-scene cluster. Mechanistically distinct from the pre-traversal disconnection cluster: stair crossing succeeds and failure occurs during post-climb floor-2 exploration. look_for_downstair never fires as a stall mode — the stair is traversed via get_close_to_stair→climb_success — making all look_for_downstair interventions structurally unreachable. The disconnection is within floor 2 (goal room isolated from landing), not at the stair boundary.
clusters.transfer_notes: candidate_8's pre-entry pathfinder gate was designed for q3zU7Yy5E5s/qyAac8rV8Zk and confirmed no-op for XB4GS9ShBRE for a categorically different reason: the gate's FSM entry point is never reached because look_for_downstair never activates. This proves the two navmesh-disconnection clusters require independent fixes and share no causal path. Identical behavioral fingerprint across all 8 candidates confirms zero overlap with any tested mechanism.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: frontier_seeding_beyond_stair_landing_via_navmesh_island_radius: after floor entry, call pathfinder.island_radius(stair_landing_pos) to measure the accessible island; if island area <50m², sample additional synthetic frontier candidates via get_random_navigable_point_near() in a 5m radius, seed them into the frontier pool to extend exploration beyond the landing; if floor exhaustion occurs within 30 floor_steps, fall back to retry a different floor entry point by searching for alternative navigable stair-adjacent positions via exhaustive navmesh island survey

forbidden_moves.reason: Globally mandated forbidden move across all scenes and clusters per explicit constraint
Globally mandated forbidden move across all scenes and clusters per explicit constraint
Directly caused the mL8ThkuaVTM regression by converting passive climb_stair into get_close_to_stair→look_for_downstair oscillation; floor reinits rose from 3 to 6
Ruled out for both q3zU7Yy5E5s and qyAac8rV8Zk; does not address navmesh connectivity; empirically confirmed to regress mL8ThkuaVTM, creating a cross-cluster cost with no benefit

look_for_downstair exits naturally in 2–12 steps in both scenes; gate silently falls through try/except or pathfinder.find_path() node-snaps to false-feasible; zero behavioral effect confirmed by identical fingerprints in candidate_8 for both scenes
XB4GS9ShBRE traverses stairs via climb_stair success; look_for_downstair never activates as a stall mode, making the gate structurally unreachable
look_for_downstair runs only 2–12 steps before natural exit in both scenes; any meaningful budget T>12 never fires; confirmed no-op in candidate_7
look_for_downstair exits naturally in ≤12 steps in both scenes, below any counter threshold N that would trigger action; confirmed no-op in candidate_6
Stair disable confirmed behavioral no-op in candidate_6: agent re-enables stair and re-enters get_close_to_stair via a separate code path not gated by the disable mechanism
Navmesh disconnection is a structural Habitat geometry constraint; no DP parameter change can make an unreachable navmesh island reachable; confirmed by identical failure across all 12-DP sweeps
Post-climb floor-2 navmesh disconnection is orthogonal to all 12 DP parameters; confirmed by identical behavioral fingerprints across all 8 candidates including full DP sweeps
